articles.description: Sergio Garcia says LIV Golf's players were told earlier this year the event would run for "many years" as rumours swirl the Saudi-backed venture is on the verge of collapse.
articles.title: LIV Golf: Sergio Garcia says players told Saudi Arabia venture would run for 'many years' amid rumours of collapse

articles.description: Man City's meeting against Arsenal may go a long way to deciding the destination of the Premier League trophy in May, but how close a finish could it be?
articles.title: Premier League title race: Could Man City and Arsenal go to a play-off?

articles.description: Andrew Benson assesses how much money is needed to reach Formula 1 and why costs have increased so much.
articles.title: F1: Do you have to be a millionaire to make it to the top of the sport?

articles.description: New Jersey Governor Mikie Sherrill accuses Fifa of failing to provide funding, which means fans must foot the bill for high transport costs at the World Cup this summer.
articles.title: World Cup 2026: Fifa blamed as train tickets set to cost $100

articles.description: The quickest strike in this season's Champions League, seven goals, two red cards and the competition's 15-time winners exiting in anger - once again the knockout stages of European club football's elite tournament has delivered drama in spades.
articles.title: Bayern Munich vs Real Madrid: Giants deliver Champions League classic

articles.description: After losing out to Bayern Munich in a thrilling Champions League quarter-final, Real Madrid face the prospect of a trophyless season that could cost Alvaro Arbeloa his job.
articles.title: Real Madrid set for rare trophyless season - will Arbeloa pay the price?

articles.description: Seven-time winner Ronnie O'Sullivan begins his bid for a record-breaking eighth World Snooker Championship title with a match against China's debutant He Guoqiang at the Crucible.
articles.title: 2026 World Snooker Championship draw: Ronnie O'Sullivan to play China's He Guoqiang in Crucible round one

articles.description: Mikel Arteta wanted "fire" from his Arsenal players but instead got a sluggish performance as the Gunners did what was needed to reach the Champions League semi-finals.
articles.title: Arsenal: Mikel Arteta wanted fire but Gunners limp to semis - does style matter?

articles.description: Newcastle United spent £124m to replace departing Alexander Isak, but Eddie Howe's men now seem no closer to finding their striking solution.
articles.title: Newcastle: £124m spent but Newcastle no closer to a striker solution

articles.description: France striker Hugo Ekitike will miss the World Cup after suffering a rupture of the Achilles tendon during Liverpool's Champions League defeat by Paris St-Germain on Tuesday.
articles.title: Hugo Ekitike injury: France striker to miss to miss rest of season and World Cup with ruptured Achilles
